books.title: Paradise Lost
books.author: John Milton
books.summary: The epic poem of humanity's fall from grace, featuring Satan's rebellion against God.
books.historical_context: Written during the English Restoration, reflecting religious and political tensions of the time.

books.title: Wuthering Heights
books.author: Emily Brontë
books.summary: On the stormy Yorkshire moors, the obsessive bond between Heathcliff and Catherine Earnshaw unleashes generations of passion, cruelty, and revenge.
books.historical_context: Emily Brontë's 1847 publication challenged Victorian sensibilities with its raw passion and Byronic anti-hero.

books.title: Jane Eyre
books.author: Charlotte Brontë
books.summary: An orphaned governess finds love and independence despite social constraints and dark secrets.
books.historical_context: Published in 1847, challenged Victorian gender roles and class structures.

books.title: Pride and Prejudice
books.author: Jane Austen
books.summary: Elizabeth Bennet navigates marriage prospects and social expectations in Regency England.
books.historical_context: Austen's wit and social commentary captured the changing dynamics of English society.

books.title: Emma
books.author: Jane Austen
books.summary: A well-meaning but misguided young woman meddles in the romantic lives of those around her.
books.historical_context: Explores the social hierarchies and marriage customs of Regency England.

books.title: Sense and Sensibility
books.author: Jane Austen
books.summary: Two sisters with contrasting temperaments navigate love and loss.
books.historical_context: Austen's first published novel examining emotion versus reason in Regency society.

books.title: Great Expectations
books.author: Charles Dickens
books.summary: Pip's journey from humble blacksmith's apprentice to gentleman reveals the corruption of wealth and class.
books.historical_context: Dickens' critique of Victorian social mobility and industrial capitalism.

books.title: David Copperfield
books.author: Charles Dickens
books.summary: A young man's bildungsroman through Victorian England's changing social landscape.
books.historical_context: Dickens' most autobiographical novel, reflecting his own childhood experiences.

books.title: Oliver Twist
books.author: Charles Dickens
books.summary: An orphan boy exposes the harsh realities of workhouses and criminal underworld.
books.historical_context: Dickens' social reform novel highlighting poverty and child exploitation.

books.title: A Tale of Two Cities
books.author: Charles Dickens
books.summary: Love and sacrifice during the French Revolution's reign of terror.
books.historical_context: Dickens' historical novel examining revolution, sacrifice, and redemption.

books.title: Middlemarch
books.author: George Eliot
books.summary: Interconnected lives in a provincial English town reveal universal human struggles.
books.historical_context: Eliot's masterpiece of psychological realism in Victorian England.

books.title: Silas Marner
books.author: George Eliot
books.summary: A miserly weaver finds redemption through love and community.
books.historical_context: Explores themes of faith, community, and social change in rural England.

books.title: Tess of the d'Urbervilles
books.author: Thomas Hardy
books.summary: A pure woman's tragic fate in the hands of social hypocrisy and male dominance.
books.historical_context: Hardy's critique of Victorian moral double standards and social injustice.

books.title: The Mayor of Casterbridge
books.author: Thomas Hardy
books.summary: A man's past actions return to destroy his respectable life.
books.historical_context: Hardy's exploration of character, fate, and social change in rural England.

books.title: Jude the Obscure
books.author: Thomas Hardy
books.summary: A working-class man's frustrated ambitions and unconventional relationships.
books.historical_context: Hardy's controversial final novel challenged marriage and educational conventions.

books.title: Far from the Madding Crowd
books.author: Thomas Hardy
books.summary: Bathsheba Everdene's independence and romantic entanglements in rural England.
books.historical_context: Hardy's pastoral novel examining rural life and women's autonomy.

books.title: Frankenstein
books.author: Mary Shelley
books.summary: A scientist's creation of artificial life leads to tragedy and moral questions.
books.historical_context: Gothic novel exploring scientific ethics and human nature during the Industrial Revolution.

books.title: Dr. Jekyll and Mr. Hyde
books.author: Robert Louis Stevenson
books.summary: A respected doctor's experiments reveal the duality of human nature.
books.historical_context: Victorian exploration of moral psychology and urban anxiety.

books.title: Treasure Island
books.author: Robert Louis Stevenson
books.summary: Young Jim Hawkins searches for pirate treasure on a mysterious island.
books.historical_context: Classic adventure novel capturing the golden age of piracy.

books.title: Kidnapped
books.author: Robert Louis Stevenson
books.summary: David Balfour's adventures in 18th-century Scotland after being betrayed by his uncle.
books.historical_context: Historical adventure set in post-Jacobite Scotland.

books.title: Dracula
books.author: Bram Stoker
books.summary: Count Dracula's invasion of England and the fight to stop him.
books.historical_context: Gothic horror novel reflecting Victorian anxieties about sexuality and foreign influence.

books.title: The Picture of Dorian Gray
books.author: Oscar Wilde
books.summary: A young man's portrait ages while he remains eternally youthful and morally corrupt.
books.historical_context: Wilde's decadent novel exploring art, beauty, and moral corruption in Victorian society.

books.title: The Importance of Being Earnest
books.author: Oscar Wilde
books.summary: Witty comedy of manners about mistaken identities and social pretensions.
books.historical_context: Wilde's satirical masterpiece mocking Victorian social conventions.

books.title: Heart of Darkness
books.author: Joseph Conrad
books.summary: Marlow's journey into the African interior reveals the horror of imperialism.
books.historical_context: Conrad's critique of European colonialism and its psychological effects.

books.title: Lord Jim
books.author: Joseph Conrad
books.summary: A young officer seeks redemption after abandoning his ship.
books.historical_context: Conrad's exploration of honor, guilt, and colonialism in the East Indies.

books.title: The Turn of the Screw
books.author: Henry James
books.summary: A governess encounters supernatural threats to the children in her care.
books.historical_context: James' psychological ghost story exploring perception and reality.

books.title: The Portrait of a Lady
books.author: Henry James
books.summary: Isabel Archer's quest for independence leads to entrapment in a loveless marriage.
books.historical_context: James' international novel examining American innocence versus European sophistication.

books.title: The Wings of the Dove
books.author: Henry James
books.summary: Love, deception, and moral complexity in a triangle involving a dying heiress.
books.historical_context: James' late masterpiece exploring the psychology of relationships and manipulation.

books.title: The Ambassadors
books.author: Henry James
books.summary: Lambert Strether's mission to Paris transforms his understanding of life and culture.
books.historical_context: James' exploration of American versus European values and personal awakening.

books.title: Kim
books.author: Rudyard Kipling
books.summary: An orphaned Irish boy becomes a spy in British India's Great Game.
books.historical_context: Kipling's novel of imperial adventure and cultural collision in colonial India.

books.title: The Jungle Book
books.author: Rudyard Kipling
books.summary: Mowgli's adventures growing up among wolves in the Indian jungle.
books.historical_context: Kipling's tales of colonial India blending adventure with moral instruction.

books.title: The Strange Case of Dr. Jekyll and Mr. Hyde
books.author: Robert Louis Stevenson
books.summary: A respected doctor's experiments with his dark side have terrifying consequences.
books.historical_context: Victorian Gothic exploring the duality of human nature and urban alienation.

books.title: The Time Machine
books.author: H.G. Wells
books.summary: A Victorian inventor travels to the year 802,701 and discovers humanity's disturbing future.
books.historical_context: Wells' scientific romance exploring class division and evolutionary theory.

books.title: The War of the Worlds
books.author: H.G. Wells
books.summary: Martians invade Earth with devastating technology, challenging human supremacy.
books.historical_context: Wells' invasion narrative reflecting imperial anxieties and scientific progress.

books.title: The Invisible Man
books.author: H.G. Wells
books.summary: A scientist's invisibility experiment leads to madness and terror.
books.historical_context: Wells' science fiction exploring the dangers of unchecked scientific ambition.

books.title: The Island of Dr. Moreau
books.author: H.G. Wells
books.summary: A castaway discovers a mad scientist's experiments transforming animals into humans.
books.historical_context: Wells' horror exploring evolution, vivisection, and the boundaries of humanity.

books.title: The Adventures of Sherlock Holmes
books.author: Arthur Conan Doyle
books.summary: The brilliant detective and his loyal companion solve crimes in Victorian London.
books.historical_context: Doyle's detective fiction reflecting urban anxieties and scientific rationalism.

books.title: The Hound of the Baskervilles
books.author: Arthur Conan Doyle
books.summary: Holmes investigates a supernatural curse threatening the Baskerville family.
books.historical_context: Gothic mystery combining detective fiction with supernatural elements.

books.title: Alice's Adventures in Wonderland
books.author: Lewis Carroll
books.summary: Alice falls down a rabbit hole into a nonsensical world of talking animals and absurd logic.
books.historical_context: Carroll's children's literature challenging Victorian rationality through fantasy and wordplay.

books.title: Through the Looking-Glass
books.author: Lewis Carroll
books.summary: Alice enters a mirror world where everything is reversed and logic inverted.
books.historical_context: Carroll's sequel exploring themes of growing up and mathematical concepts.

books.title: The Wind in the Willows
books.author: Kenneth Grahame
books.summary: Animal friends Mole, Rat, Badger, and Toad have adventures along the riverbank.
books.historical_context: Grahame's children's classic celebrating rural England and friendship.

books.title: Peter Pan
books.author: J.M. Barrie
books.summary: The boy who wouldn't grow up takes the Darling children to his magical island.
books.historical_context: Barrie's fantasy exploring childhood, imagination, and the fear of growing up.

books.title: The Secret Garden
books.author: Frances Hodgson Burnett
books.summary: A spoiled girl discovers healing and friendship in a hidden garden.
books.historical_context: Burnett's children's novel exploring themes of renewal and the healing power of nature.

books.title: Little Women
books.author: Louisa May Alcott
books.summary: The March sisters grow up during the American Civil War era.
books.historical_context: Alcott's coming-of-age story reflecting women's changing roles in 19th-century America.

books.title: Anne of Green Gables
books.author: L.M. Montgomery
books.summary: An orphaned girl with a vivid imagination finds a home with elderly siblings.
books.historical_context: Montgomery's beloved children's novel celebrating rural Canadian life and childhood.

books.title: The Call of the Wild
books.author: Jack London
books.summary: Buck, a domesticated dog, answers the call of the wilderness during the Gold Rush.
books.historical_context: London's adventure novel reflecting the harsh realities of the Klondike Gold Rush.

books.title: White Fang
books.author: Jack London
books.summary: A wolf-dog's journey from the wild to domestication explores nature versus nurture.
books.historical_context: London's companion to The Call of the Wild, examining civilization's impact on wild nature.

books.title: The Adventures of Tom Sawyer
books.author: Mark Twain
books.summary: A mischievous boy's adventures along the Mississippi River capture American childhood.
books.historical_context: Twain's nostalgic portrayal of antebellum America and childhood innocence.

books.title: Adventures of Huckleberry Finn
books.author: Mark Twain
books.summary: Huck's journey down the Mississippi with escaped slave Jim explores freedom and friendship.
books.historical_context: Twain's masterpiece confronting racism and moral growth in post-Civil War America.

books.title: The Prince and the Pauper
books.author: Mark Twain
books.summary: A prince and a poor boy switch places, learning about class and identity.
books.historical_context: Twain's historical fiction exploring social inequality and justice.

books.title: A Connecticut Yankee in King Arthur's Court
books.author: Mark Twain
books.summary: A modern American transported to medieval England brings technology and democracy.
books.historical_context: Twain's satire comparing American progress with medieval superstition.
